hero.titleLead: The Dofus overlay that fits behind
hero.subtitle: 185 dungeons, bilingual FR/EN strategies, a short form you can read in 10 seconds. No Alt+Tab, no ads, no tracking.
hero.microCopy: Windows 10/11 · WebView2 required (bundled on W11) · Works with Dofus 2.x and 3.x

resultBullets: Block regeneration on turn 1 with Slow Ray.
Never hit the shadow while the shield is up.
Dispel before the charge or it's a wipe.

valueProps.sectionTitle: Three things that change your run

items.title: Instant
items.body: Global Alt+D, open in under 150 ms. Zero alt-tab, zero game switch.

items.title: Actionable
items.body: 3 to 6 colored bullets per boss. Designed to be read mid-fight, not in passive reading.

items.title: Transparent
items.body: Every sentence is traceable — official, community, or LLM with a citation. Zero hallucinations tolerated.

items.title: Two views per boss
items.body: Actionable for mid-fight, detailed when you want to understand a cycle. Keyboard toggle between them.

items.title: FR ⇄ EN in one click
items.body: Instant language toggle with Ctrl+L. Both languages are on par — no second-class EN content.

items.title: Verified data
items.body: Every line carries a provenance badge: official Ankama, consolidated community, or LLM with a source citation. Nothing is made up.

items.title: Endgame coverage 160+
items.body: 114 dungeons above level 160, 100 of them with a full strategy. Continuously updated via public PRs.

steps.title: Run the setup
steps.body: Accept WebView2 if prompted — standard Microsoft component, bundled on Windows 11.

steps.title: Alt+D in-game
steps.body: On top of Dofus. Search, read, play. No account, no data sent.

roadmap.title: Public, versioned, PR-driven
roadmap.subtitle: Most up-voted issues ship first. Everything is tracked.

columns.label: In progress
columns.body: Endgame coverage, EN i18n, contributor workflow.

columns.label: Next
columns.body: Builds view, advanced search, low-latency mode.

columns.label: Community asks
columns.body: Suggest, upvote, discuss. Popular requests move up.

support.title: Maintained by one person in their spare time
support.body: If the tool helps you, a sponsor or a star on the repo makes a real difference.

credits.title: The people who made this happen

items.name: Kamil F.
items.body: Dofus Companion wouldn't exist without Kamil. A long-time Dofus fan, he came up with the tool and carried the vision: what an endgame player actually wants to read mid-fight, and what they definitely don't. The whole '3 to 6 actionable bullets' philosophy comes from him.

items.name: noureddinestam + Claude
items.body: Vibe-coded: Noureddine drives, Claude types the code, fast iteration by conversation. From the landing page to the Tauri overlay, the entire repo was built at that tempo.

footer.tagline: Open source Windows overlay. MIT license.

disclaimer.body: Dofus Companion is an unofficial application, built by fans for fans. Dofus® is a registered trademark of Ankama Games. This project is neither endorsed by nor affiliated with Ankama. No memory reading, no game automation — this is an information overlay only.

previous.title: Other versions

smartScreen.title: If Windows shows a warning, here's why and what to do
smartScreen.body: The app is open source and signed with the Tauri signature (for the auto-updater), but without a Windows EV certificate. Until the app has enough reputation-building downloads, SmartScreen may show "Windows protected your PC".
smartScreen.microCopy: You can verify the SHA256 hash before running, from the PowerShell block below.

virusTotal.label: VirusTotal
virusTotal.notSubmitted: The file has not been submitted to VirusTotal yet.

security.title: Security and transparency
security.subtitle: What the app does, what it doesn't, and how to verify it yourself.

behavior.title: What the app does and doesn't

positive.heading: What it does
items: Reads local JSON files (the dungeon data).
Displays a window on top of Dofus.
Checks GitHub Releases for auto-update (HTTPS, Ed25519 signature).

negative.heading: What it doesn't
items: No memory reading of the Dofus process (Ankama ToS-compliant).
No keyboard or mouse input simulation (not a bot).
No telemetry, no analytics, no tracking.

fingerprints.title: SHA256 fingerprints
fingerprints.body: Asset SHA256 hashes will be published in an upcoming release (SHA256SUMS.txt attached). In the meantime, you can verify locally from PowerShell.

virusTotal.title: VirusTotal
virusTotal.body: Each asset is submitted to VirusTotal (70+ antivirus engines). The badge below shows the live result and links to the full report. An open source installer without an EV certificate may occasionally trigger a false positive on a heuristic (machine learning) engine — this does not affect binary integrity, and clears with project reputation or an EV certificate.

smartScreen.title: Windows SmartScreen
smartScreen.body: SmartScreen blocks executables without enough reputation. The app is Tauri-signed but lacks a Windows EV certificate. The warning will disappear with enough downloads or via an EV certificate. If you'd rather, you can build from source.

report.title: Report a vulnerability
report.body: Open a Security Advisory on GitHub. Response within 72h, coordinated fix and disclosure.

changelog.title: Release history
changelog.subtitle: Every version, its changes, and links to the full notes and binaries.
changelog.unavailable: Couldn't load releases from GitHub — try again later.

items.a: Dofus Companion respects Ankama's ToS: no process memory reading, no keyboard/mouse automation, no network packet reading. It's an information overlay — you search, you read, you play. Not affiliated with Ankama, not endorsed by Ankama.
The app doesn't interact with Dofus. No known ban cases linked to an information overlay. That said, Ankama is the sole judge — at your own risk.
A bot plays for you (macros, automation, memory reading). Dofus Companion displays strategies on top of the game window. You read, you apply them yourself.

items.q: Windows SmartScreen blocks the setup. What now?
items.a: That's normal — the app doesn't have a Windows EV certificate yet. Click "More info" then "Run anyway". You can verify the SHA256 hash first, from the /download page.

items.q: My antivirus flags a false positive
items.a: Check the file's SHA256 on /download and compare with the VirusTotal report. If 0 detections on VirusTotal, it's a local false positive — report it to your antivirus vendor.
In %APPDATA%\Dofus Companion (Windows). Local JSON data only — nothing is sent to a server.
Check that no other app (Discord, AutoHotkey, OBS) is using the same shortcut. You can change the hotkey in the app's settings.

items.q: The overlay disappears in exclusive fullscreen
items.a: Dofus in exclusive fullscreen hides Windows overlays. Switch Dofus to "Borderless window" in the game's graphics settings.

items.q: How do I change the shortcut?
items.a: Right-click the app icon → Settings → Global shortcut. Any free combination works.
Nowhere. The app reads local JSON files and checks GitHub Releases for auto-update (HTTPS + Ed25519 signature). No telemetry, no analytics, no tracking.
Windows Control Panel → Apps → Dofus Companion → Uninstall. The NSIS installer removes the data folder. For MSI: same thing via Apps & features.

items.q: Are the strategies up to date?
items.a: Continuously updated via public PRs. Every sentence carries a provenance badge (official, community, LLM + citation). You can report an error via GitHub Issues.

items.q: How do I report a mistake in a strategy?
items.a: Open an Issue on GitHub with the boss name and what's incorrect. See the Contribute page.

items.q: How do I translate or add a language?
items.a: EN is already covered. For another language, open a GitHub Discussion — we'll scope it together first.

contribute.title: The project moves forward with you
contribute.subtitle: Code, data, reports — everything goes through GitHub, tracked and versioned.

paths.title: Code
paths.body: Fork, PR, issue. Conventional Commits. README at the repo root for local setup.

paths.title: Data
paths.body: Fix or enrich dungeon strategies. Workflow documented in DATA-CONTRIBUTING.md.

paths.title: Bug report
paths.body: Dedicated issue template with the useful info: version, OS, hotkey, repro steps.

paths.title: Discussion
paths.body: An idea? A question that doesn't fit an issue? GitHub Discussions is the place.

supportPage.title: Maintained by one person in their spare time
supportPage.body: If Dofus Companion helps you, a GitHub sponsor or a star on the repo makes a real difference.

sections.heading: What data do we collect?
sections.body: None. The local application doesn't collect, store, or send any personal data. The strategy JSON files are read only from your local disk.

sections.heading: Cookies?
sections.body: No cookies on this site. No tracker, no analytics.

sections.heading: Server logs
sections.body: Vercel's standard access logs (IP, user-agent, path, status code) are retained per their default policy. We neither consult nor export them.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: For any privacy-related question, open a Discussion or a Security Advisory on GitHub.

sections.heading: License
sections.body: Dofus Companion is distributed under the MIT license. You may use, modify, and redistribute it under the license terms (see LICENSE in the repo).

sections.heading: Warranty
sections.body: The software is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind. Use at your own risk.

sections.heading: Dofus trademark
sections.body: Dofus® is a registered trademark of Ankama Games. Dofus Companion is an unofficial application, built by fans for fans, not affiliated with Ankama.
